description: One-pass review of crate secretx-env (/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretsx/secretx-env). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
RETRACTION: claim that std::env::var panics on names containing '=' or NUL is FALSE. Verified by rustc execution: it returns Err(NotPresent). Updated finding crate-secretsx-3btv.1 to reflect actual (weaker) behaviour: silent NotFound for syntactically impossible names. Lesson: verify std behaviour rather than trust memory of edge cases.
Maintainer risk: LOW — surface area is one file, 65 LoC excluding tests; the logic is mechanical (look up an env var). Real risks are documentation (zeroize semantics) and one outlier test that does not match workspace unsafe conventions.
Correctness confidence: 90% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: none — the surface is too small to harbour subtle correctness issues. Uncertainty driven by: I did not exercise the inventory::submit! macro expansion to verify factory dispatch through the umbrella crate; the registration could in principle have a name collision or shadowing issue I cannot see from this crate alone.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code is small, mechanical, and idiomatic for its size. The only non-opinion finding is the missing Debug derive (idiomatic-rust,traits).
Rust idiom confidence: 95% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: none. Uncertainty driven by: I did not look at how the umbrella crate's dispatcher interacts with this backend's registration — there could be a hygiene or lifetime issue at the inventory::collect! → submit! seam that is invisible from inside this crate.
title: Review: secretx-env rusty pass
